response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior developer with strong polyglot experience, geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective, and low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic. However, the role specifically requires modern C++ (or Rust) as the primary language, and the resume shows only brief, dated C/C++ work at National Instruments (2014-2015) with no recent commercial C++ or Rust experience. Most recent work is TypeScript/Node.js. Worth a screening call given the geospatial background and systems aptitude, but the core language gap is significant.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map work
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of req/s)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, Linux
concerns: No recent C++ or Rust experience; last C/C++ role was over a decade ago
Primary modern stack is TypeScript/Node.js, not systems languages
No demonstrated experience with routing tile pipelines or navigation guidance systems
